prevalence.value: ~4-10% (adults; higher in women and older adults)
prevalence.trend: stable; detection increases with routine TSH testing

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; potential progression to overt hypothyroidism

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Elevated TSH with normal free T4; repeat testing to confirm and assess thyroid antibodies

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening; testing in symptomatic or high-risk patients
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with monitoring and selective treatment

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: TSH testing widely available; antibody testing in referral centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Observation for mild cases; levothyroxine for symptomatic, pregnant, or high TSH
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overtreatment in low-risk patients and follow-up gaps
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear guideline thresholds and access to low-cost levothyroxine
